summary: Small decompilation matching improvement in Pikachu Thunder item spawning. The PR adjusted local variable declaration order and lifetimes in `it_802B1DF8` without intending behavior changes, improving the function match from 97.5% to 98.27586% while keeping the function size at 464/464 bytes.
what_changed: In `it_802B1DF8`, several local declarations were reordered to better match generated code.
`Item_GObj* item_gobj` was moved from a loop-local declaration to an outer function-scope declaration.
`Item* ip` and `itPikachuthunderAttributes* attrs` were moved from early function-scope declarations into the narrower `if (item_gobj != NULL)` block.
`first` was declared earlier alongside `item_gobj`, while `i` was moved lower in the declaration list.
No item spawn logic, linked Thunder segment behavior, delay handling, or field assignments were intentionally changed.
smart_moves: Targeted only local variable lifetimes/order instead of rewriting logic, reducing behavioral risk while improving the match.
Kept `ip` and `attrs` scoped close to their actual use, while hoisting `item_gobj` to function scope where the compiler match apparently required it.
Preserved function size at 464/464 bytes, indicating the change improved instruction/register matching rather than changing emitted code size.
Verified with both a focused object build and `objdiff-cli` against `main/melee/it/items/itpikachuthunder`.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn partial matches, local declaration order and scope can affect register allocation and stack layout even when source behavior is identical.
Narrowing some locals while widening others can be the right combination; do not assume all variables should be declared at first use for matching.
When improving an already mostly-matching function, prefer minimal local-lifetime experiments over semantic rewrites.
Documenting before/after match percentages and function byte size helps reviewers confirm a PR is a matching-only improvement.
naming_conventions: `Item_GObj*` locals use names such as `owner`, `prev`, `first`, and `item_gobj`.
Item object pointers are named `ip` after `GET_ITEM(item_gobj)`.
Article special attributes are represented as `itPikachuthunderAttributes* attrs`.
Spawn setup uses a `SpawnItem spawn` local and fields such as `spawn.kind`, `spawn.prev_pos`, and `spawn.x40`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reorder local declarations to influence compiler output without changing control flow.
Adjust variable lifetime by moving declarations between function scope, loop scope, and inner block scope.
Hoist `item_gobj` out of the loop-local declaration to affect lifetime/register allocation.
Move `ip` and `attrs` declarations into the `item_gobj != NULL` block to shorten their lifetimes.
Use `objdiff-cli diff -p . -u main/melee/it/items/itpikachuthunder` to validate the matching improvement.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly states no behavior change was intended.
follow_up_queries: Find other item spawn functions where moving loop-local `Item_GObj*` variables to outer scope improves matching.
Search for Pikachu Thunder item functions with remaining partial matches in `src/melee/it/items/itpikachuthunder.c`.
Look for functions where `Item* ip` and attributes pointers should be declared inside null-check blocks for better register allocation.
Compare other PRs that improved matches solely through local variable lifetime or declaration-order changes.
